description_long: This rifle features a long, blued steel barrel and a classic lever-action mechanism, reminiscent of late 19th-century Winchester designs. The stock and fore-end are crafted from polished wood, likely walnut, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and a warm brown hue. The receiver and lever are metallic, with a matte gray-green tint, and the trigger guard is integrated into the lever loop. Iron sights are mounted on the barrel, with no visible scope or advanced optics. The magazine appears to be tubular, running beneath the barrel, typical of lever-action rifles. The overall build is robust, with a traditional fixed wooden stock and a long, slender profile.
description: A classic lever-action rifle with a long blued barrel, polished wooden stock, and tubular magazine beneath the barrel. Features iron sights and a robust, traditional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow muzzle, tubular magazine, and the rounded end of the wooden fore-end.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the barrel, lever-action mechanism, and the wooden stock.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and receiver.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the lever, trigger guard, and the continuous wooden stock and fore-end.
